subject: Roif (2025a/b) Akkadian shorthand model validation
corpus: Holdat LLC v3 (1670 seals) + Phase-142/143/135 reports

claim_scores.description: Fish sign functions as occupational/title marker (not commodity counter)
claim_scores.roif_prediction: Fish sign (M047) will be INITIAL-dominant (title), never isolated
claim_scores.detail: 100% compound across all sites including Lothal (coastal port)

claim_scores.description: Signs have context-dependent meanings (Akkadian shorthand = polysemy)
claim_scores.roif_prediction: Same sign should show divergent left/right neighbor profiles by positional slot
claim_scores.detail: M267 strongest: 81% MEDIAL in compound context (genitive particle)

claim_scores.description: Animal icons and INITIAL signs co-select to encode guild professional identity
claim_scores.roif_prediction: Specific INITIAL signs should be significantly enriched with specific animal icons
claim_scores.detail: Strongest: M045(yānai)×elephant chi2=155, M062(erutu)×zebu chi2=84, M060(rhinoceros)×rhinoceros chi2=158

claim_scores.description: Script encodes professional identity, not commodity quantities
claim_scores.roif_prediction: Sign combinations encode [TITLE][NAME][CASE] — identity, not tally marks
claim_scores.observed_value: Grammar model [INITIAL][MEDIAL][TERMINAL] confirmed R²=0.992 vs 0.438 shuffled (z=10.3)
claim_scores.detail: All 15 top blocking signs are MEDIAL (personal name components) — exactly what identity system predicts

claim_scores.description: Sign compounds encode professional role, not commodity quantities
claim_scores.roif_prediction: Fish signs (maritime markers) will never appear as solitary quantity markers
claim_scores.detail: 0% isolation is consistent with compound professional identity, not tally marks

claim_scores.description: Highest-frequency bigram represents a title compound formula
claim_scores.roif_prediction: Most common sign pair should have high PMI and represent a recurring title
claim_scores.observed_value: Top bigram M342·M176: count=122 (7.3% of seals), PMI=2.432
claim_scores.detail: M342·M176 (ay/ā · an/aṇ) = masculine genitive suffix compound — personal name formula

claim_scores.description: M267 is a genitive/possessive particle creating 'X of Y' title constructions
claim_scores.roif_prediction: M267 should be MEDIAL (between title and name) with high frequency
claim_scores.detail: Phase-142D: M267 is 81% MEDIAL in compound context → genitive reading strongly supported

claim_scores.description: Different sites reflect different trade specializations (guild/commodity variation by site)
claim_scores.roif_prediction: Maritime trade sites (Chanhu-daro) should show different sign profiles from inland admin sites
claim_scores.observed_value: Max site KL divergence=0.708; most divergent pair=Chanhu-daro|Rakhigarhi
claim_scores.detail: Chanhu-daro (maritime port) vs Rakhigarhi (admin hub): KL=0.708 — functionally distinct repertoires

key_findings: Roif model overall verdict: STRONGLY_SUPPORTED (100.0% weighted support)
R1 (fish=title marker): CONFIRMED — 100% compound, M047 INITIAL-dominant
R3 (icon×guild co-selection): CONFIRMED — 63 enriched INITIAL×icon pairs
R4 (identity not tally): CONFIRMED — grammar R²=0.992, all blockers MEDIAL (name slots)
R7 (M267 genitive): CONFIRMED — 81% MEDIAL in compound context
R8 (site specialization): CONFIRMED — KL=0.708 maritime vs administrative sites
implications_for_collaboration: All core structural predictions of Roif's shorthand model are confirmed. The main remaining question is whether his specific Akkadian phonetic mappings align with our DEDR-grounded Dravidian readings — this is testable with the compound-context listing already shared. His guild/commodity/trade framework is the strongest external interpretive model consistent with our positional data.
